title: LINCOLN COUNTY CRISIS LINE - 211info
text: LINCOLN COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH LINCOLN COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Crisis and triage services include follow-up care after a crisis and brief therapy, after-hours counselor contact, hospitalization for acute mental health issues, and civil commitments from court proceeding for those who cannot care for their needs or are a danger to themselves or others. 51 SW Lee Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (866) 266-0288 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Hotline (866) 266-0288 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for crisis services FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by language line AREA SERVED: Lincoln County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: General Crisis Intervention Hotlines Mental Health Crisis Lines Suicide Prevention Hotlines Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LINCOLN COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH
